ingredients.name: Niacinamide
ingredients.reason: Brightens skin tone, reduces pore appearance, strengthens skin barrier, anti-inflammatory. Well tolerated by most skin types including sensitive and acne-prone skin.
ingredients.notes: One of the most versatile and well-researched skincare ingredients. Effective at concentrations of 2-10%.

ingredients.name: Hyaluronic Acid
ingredients.reason: Powerful humectant that attracts and retains moisture in the skin. Plumps and hydrates without clogging pores.
ingredients.notes: Works best applied to damp skin. Sodium hyaluronate is a smaller molecule that penetrates deeper.

ingredients.name: Glycolic Acid
ingredients.reason: AHA exfoliant that removes dead skin cells, improves texture, fades hyperpigmentation and stimulates collagen production.
ingredients.notes: Increases sun sensitivity — always use SPF. Start at low concentrations (5-8%). Not recommended for sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Salicylic Acid
ingredients.reason: Oil-soluble BHA exfoliant that penetrates pores, dissolves sebum plugs, anti-inflammatory. Excellent for acne-prone and oily skin.
ingredients.notes: Avoid if allergic to aspirin. Use SPF. Effective at 0.5-2% concentrations.

ingredients.name: Retinol
ingredients.reason: Gold standard anti-aging ingredient. Increases cell turnover, stimulates collagen, fades dark spots and improves skin texture.
ingredients.notes: Start low and slow — 0.025% to 0.1%. Can cause purging, dryness and irritation initially. Avoid during pregnancy. Always use SPF.

ingredients.name: Vitamin C
ingredients.reason: Potent antioxidant that brightens skin, fades hyperpigmentation, boosts collagen synthesis and protects against UV damage.
ingredients.notes: Pure L-ascorbic acid is most effective but unstable — look for formulas below pH 3.5. Store away from light and air.

ingredients.name: Azelaic Acid
ingredients.reason: Antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and brightening. Effective for acne, rosacea and post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation.
ingredients.notes: One of the few actives safe during pregnancy. Available OTC at 10% and prescription at 15-20%. Very well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Ceramides
ingredients.reason: Essential lipids that form the skin barrier. Restore and maintain moisture retention, protect against environmental damage.
ingredients.notes: Especially beneficial for dry, sensitive, eczema-prone skin. Most effective when combined with cholesterol and fatty acids.

ingredients.name: Peptides
ingredients.reason: Signal proteins that stimulate collagen and elastin production. Anti-aging, firming and barrier-supportive.
ingredients.notes: Work best in leave-on products. Rinse-off products don't allow adequate absorption time.

ingredients.name: Centella Asiatica
ingredients.reason: Potent anti-inflammatory and wound-healing herb. Soothes irritated skin, strengthens barrier, stimulates collagen.
ingredients.notes: Popular in Korean skincare. Excellent for sensitive, compromised or post-procedure skin.

ingredients.name: Green Tea Extract
ingredients.reason: Rich in antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, reduce inflammation and protect against UV-induced damage.
ingredients.notes: Anti-aging and anti-acne properties. Works well combined with other antioxidants.

ingredients.name: Licorice Root Extract
ingredients.reason: Brightening and anti-inflammatory. Inhibits melanin production, fades dark spots and soothes redness.
ingredients.notes: Gentler alternative to hydroquinone for hyperpigmentation. Also has antioxidant properties.

ingredients.name: Alpha Arbutin
ingredients.reason: Tyrosinase inhibitor that reduces melanin production. Effective brightening agent for hyperpigmentation and dark spots.
ingredients.notes: More stable and effective than beta-arbutin. Gentler than hydroquinone. Effective at 1-2%.

ingredients.name: Tranexamic Acid
ingredients.reason: Brightening agent that targets melasma, post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation and uneven skin tone.
ingredients.notes: Originally a pharmaceutical hemostatic agent. Increasingly used in skincare at 2-5%. Well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Panthenol
ingredients.reason: Humectant and skin-restoring ingredient. Deeply hydrating, promotes wound healing, reduces inflammation.
ingredients.notes: Converts to pantothenic acid in skin. Excellent for dry, irritated or damaged skin. Very well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Allantoin
ingredients.reason: Soothing, healing and keratolytic agent. Softens skin, promotes cell regeneration, calms irritation.
ingredients.notes: Derived from comfrey plant. Excellent for sensitive and irritated skin. Enhances penetration of other ingredients.

ingredients.name: Squalane
ingredients.reason: Lightweight emollient that mimics skin's natural sebum. Deeply moisturizing, antioxidant, non-greasy.
ingredients.notes: Squalane (saturated) is stable. Squalene (unsaturated) oxidizes and can be comedogenic. Excellent for all skin types.

ingredients.name: Jojoba Oil
ingredients.reason: Technically a liquid wax that closely resembles skin's sebum. Balancing, non-greasy, antibacterial properties.
ingredients.notes: Suitable for oily and acne-prone skin due to its sebum-balancing properties. Stable and long shelf life.

ingredients.name: Rosehip Oil
ingredients.reason: Rich in vitamin A, C and essential fatty acids. Anti-aging, brightening, improves skin texture and scars.
ingredients.notes: Can oxidize quickly — store in a cool dark place. Best used at night. Patch test recommended for acne-prone skin.

ingredients.name: Sunflower Seed Oil
ingredients.reason: Rich in linoleic acid which strengthens the skin barrier. Lightweight, non-comedogenic and soothing.
ingredients.notes: High linoleic acid content makes it particularly beneficial for acne-prone skin. Very gentle and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Coconut Oil
ingredients.reason: Highly comedogenic — clogs pores and triggers breakouts in most skin types, especially acne-prone and oily skin.
ingredients.notes: Despite its popularity, coconut oil is one of the most comedogenic oils available. Fine for hair and body but avoid on face.

ingredients.name: Cocoa Butter
ingredients.reason: Highly comedogenic. Clogs pores and causes breakouts. Heavy occlusive that traps debris in pores.
ingredients.notes: Fine for body moisturization on non-acne-prone areas. Avoid on face and chest if acne-prone.

ingredients.name: Isopropyl Myristate
ingredients.reason: One of the most comedogenic ingredients in cosmetics. Penetration enhancer that carries other ingredients deep into pores, causing blockages.
ingredients.notes: Rated 5/5 on comedogenic scale. Commonly found in foundations and heavy moisturizers. Avoid entirely if acne-prone.

ingredients.name: Acetylated Lanolin
ingredients.reason: Highly comedogenic derivative of lanolin. Clogs pores and can cause milia and breakouts.
ingredients.notes: Regular lanolin is less comedogenic than acetylated forms. Common allergen — can cause contact dermatitis.

ingredients.name: Algae Extract
ingredients.reason: Certain algae extracts are highly comedogenic and can trigger breakouts in acne-prone skin.
ingredients.notes: Not all algae are equal — some are beneficial. However without knowing the specific type, best avoided by acne-prone skin.

ingredients.name: Carrageenan
ingredients.reason: Can be comedogenic and has been linked to inflammation in some studies. Used as a thickener in many products.
ingredients.notes: Controversial ingredient. Moderately comedogenic. Those with acne-prone skin should approach with caution.

ingredients.name: Heavy Mineral Oil
ingredients.reason: Heavy occlusive that forms a thick barrier trapping dirt and bacteria in pores. Can cause breakouts and milia.
ingredients.notes: Light mineral oil is less comedogenic. Heavy grades are problematic for acne-prone skin. Petroleum-derived.

ingredients.name: Isopropyl Palmitate
ingredients.reason: Highly comedogenic emollient. Clogs pores and triggers breakouts, particularly in acne-prone skin.
ingredients.notes: Common in foundations and heavy creams. Check ingredient labels carefully if acne-prone.

ingredients.name: Myristyl Myristate
ingredients.reason: Rated maximum 5/5 on comedogenic scale. One of the most pore-clogging ingredients in cosmetic formulations.
ingredients.notes: Used as an emollient and thickener. Completely avoid if acne-prone or sensitive.

ingredients.name: Palm Oil
ingredients.reason: Highly comedogenic oil that clogs pores. High in palmitic acid which can disrupt the skin barrier.
ingredients.notes: Also an environmental concern due to deforestation. Avoid on acne-prone skin.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate
ingredients.reason: Harsh detergent that strips skin of natural oils, disrupts the skin barrier, causes irritation and dryness.
ingredients.notes: Very different concern from comedogenicity — SLS is an irritant and barrier disruptor. Common in foaming cleansers and shampoos.

ingredients.name: Denatured Alcohol
ingredients.reason: At high concentrations, strips skin barrier, causes dryness, irritation and long-term sensitization.
ingredients.notes: Small amounts as a delivery vehicle may be acceptable. Problematic when listed in top 5 ingredients. Especially bad for dry and sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Synthetic Fragrance
ingredients.reason: Leading cause of contact dermatitis in cosmetics. Can contain hundreds of undisclosed chemicals triggering allergic and irritant reactions.
ingredients.notes: Does not need to disclose individual components under current regulations. Avoid in leave-on products especially if sensitive.

ingredients.name: Essential Oils
ingredients.reason: At high concentrations, essential oils are potent irritants and common sensitizers causing contact dermatitis.
ingredients.notes: Concentration dependent — trace amounts may be acceptable. Natural does not mean safe. Particularly risky in leave-on products.

ingredients.name: Witch Hazel
ingredients.reason: Alcohol-based witch hazel strips the skin barrier and causes irritation despite its perceived toning benefits.
ingredients.notes: Alcohol-free witch hazel is less problematic. The toning effect is largely the result of skin irritation, not true improvement.

ingredients.name: Menthol
ingredients.reason: Creates a false cooling sensation while actually irritating skin. Can trigger rosacea flares and sensitization.
ingredients.notes: The cooling feeling is nerve stimulation, not skin benefit. Particularly problematic for rosacea and sensitive skin types.

ingredients.name: Peppermint Oil
ingredients.reason: High menthol content makes it a significant irritant. Causes redness, stinging and sensitization especially in sensitive skin.
ingredients.notes: Popular in lip products and scalp treatments but problematic on facial skin. Avoid if rosacea-prone.

ingredients.name: Lemon Oil
ingredients.reason: Phototoxic — causes severe UV-induced skin reactions. Highly acidic and irritating, disrupts skin barrier.
ingredients.notes: Particularly dangerous — can cause permanent hyperpigmentation when skin is exposed to sunlight. Never use before sun exposure.

ingredients.name: Lime Oil
ingredients.reason: Phototoxic citrus oil that causes UV-induced skin damage, burns and hyperpigmentation.
ingredients.notes: Same phototoxicity concerns as lemon oil. Furocoumarins in citrus oils react with UV light causing phytophotodermatitis.

ingredients.name: Cinnamon Oil
ingredients.reason: Potent skin sensitizer and irritant. Cinnamaldehyde is a well-documented contact allergen causing dermatitis.
ingredients.notes: Often marketed as a plumping agent in lip products due to irritation-induced swelling. Not a benefit — it is inflammation.

ingredients.name: Glycerin
ingredients.reason: Effective humectant that draws moisture into skin. Gentle, well tolerated and widely used across all formulations.
ingredients.notes: At very high concentrations can draw moisture from deeper skin layers in dry climates. Generally considered safe and beneficial.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.reason: Base ingredient and universal solvent in most formulations. No independent skin benefit but essential for product stability.
ingredients.notes: Usually listed first as the primary ingredient. No concerns.

ingredients.name: Propylene Glycol
ingredients.reason: Humectant and penetration enhancer. Generally safe at low concentrations but can cause irritation in sensitive individuals.
ingredients.notes: Can cause contact dermatitis in sensitive individuals at high concentrations. Generally regarded as safe in cosmetic amounts.

ingredients.name: Butylene Glycol
ingredients.reason: Humectant and solvent. Better tolerated than propylene glycol. Helps other ingredients absorb into skin.
ingredients.notes: Generally considered a gentler alternative to propylene glycol. Low irritation potential.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Alcohol
ingredients.reason: Fatty alcohol used as emollient and emulsifier. Softens skin and stabilizes formulations. Not drying like regular alcohols.
ingredients.notes: Despite the name, fatty alcohols are not the same as drying alcohols. Generally well tolerated. Mild comedogenic potential.

ingredients.name: Stearyl Alcohol
ingredients.reason: Fatty alcohol that acts as emollient and thickener. Conditions skin without the irritation of drying alcohols.
ingredients.notes: Safe for most skin types. Mild comedogenic potential — those with very acne-prone skin should be aware.

ingredients.name: Cetearyl Alcohol
ingredients.reason: Blend of cetyl and stearyl alcohols. Emollient, emulsifier and stabilizer. Conditions and softens skin.
ingredients.notes: Well tolerated by most. Mild comedogenic potential. Common in creams and lotions.

ingredients.name: Dimethicone
ingredients.reason: Silicone that creates a smooth, protective film on skin. Seals in moisture, improves texture and reduces water loss.
ingredients.notes: Not absorbed into skin. Low irritation potential. Some debate around environmental impact. Helps product spread evenly.

ingredients.name: Xanthan Gum
ingredients.reason: Natural polysaccharide used as a thickener and stabilizer. Inert and non-irritating.
ingredients.notes: No known skin concerns. Simply helps formulations achieve the right consistency.

ingredients.name: Carbomer
ingredients.reason: Synthetic polymer used as a thickener and stabilizer. Generally inert with no known skin benefit or harm.
ingredients.notes: No significant concerns. Functional ingredient that gives gels their texture.

ingredients.name: Phenoxyethanol
ingredients.reason: Common preservative that prevents microbial growth. Generally well tolerated at regulated concentrations.
ingredients.notes: Considered safe up to 1% concentration per EU regulations. Some sensitivity possible in very reactive skin types.

ingredients.name: Tocopherol
ingredients.reason: Antioxidant vitamin that neutralizes free radicals and supports skin barrier. Mildly moisturizing.
ingredients.notes: Mild comedogenic potential in pure form. Tocopheryl acetate is less likely to cause breakouts. Generally beneficial but not as potent as vitamin C.

ingredients.name: Shea Butter
ingredients.reason: Rich emollient with anti-inflammatory properties. Excellent for dry skin but may be too heavy for oily or acne-prone skin.
ingredients.notes: Comedogenic rating varies — unrefined shea butter may cause breakouts in sensitive acne-prone individuals. Highly beneficial for dry and eczema-prone skin.

ingredients.name: Aloe Vera
ingredients.reason: Soothing, hydrating and mildly anti-inflammatory. Calms sunburn and irritation. Lightweight and well tolerated.
ingredients.notes: Mostly water with some beneficial polysaccharides. Benefits are real but modest. Generally excellent for all skin types.

ingredients.name: Chamomile Extract
ingredients.reason: Anti-inflammatory and soothing botanical. Calms redness and irritation. Contains bisabolol and apigenin.
ingredients.notes: Generally well tolerated. Those with ragweed or daisy family allergies should patch test as cross-reactivity is possible.
